text: Equity, diversity, inclusion, and Indigenization (EDI&I) are widely invoked in the mission statements of institutions such as universities. Research, however, indicates little or no progress in this regard. Using a critical lens on two initiatives whose aim was to gather data related to EDI&I at the university level and narratives of experiences where members pushed back against various structural and discursive obstacles, this article identifies “institutional gaslighting” and describes four particular methods that contemporary universities employ to hinder change, such that despite openly professing their support for EDI&I discourses, they can succeed in frustrating efforts to eliminate forms of systemic bias. By theorizing the “paradox of the contemporary university”, this article also allows activism to proceed with full knowledge of the potential “obstacles” it may run into at the institutional level. Indeed, by naming such obstacles, activism can potentially counter them and arrive at the only practical answer to institutional obstruction, which is a “politics of refusal”.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
